title: Validate Model Output Schema
description: Always validate the model returned exactly what you asked for.
problem: Write validate_schema(data, required_keys, types) that checks: (1) all required_keys are present, (2) each key matches its expected type. Returns (True, None) on success or (False, error_message) on failure.
starter_code:
def validate_schema(data, required_keys, types):
    # Check all required_keys exist in data
    # Check each key matches its type in types dict
    # Return (True, None) or (False, "error message")
    return True, None  # fix this
hint: Use isinstance(data[k], types[k]) for type checking. Build a clear error message saying which key failed.
